etymology.description: 照(照) (zhào, "shine; illuminate; reflect") refers to the act of shining or projecting light, suggesting awareness and consideration. 顾(顧) (gù, "look after; attend to; care for") means to look in a certain direction or turn the head to regard something, implying attention and concern.
etymology.interpretation: 照(照) (zhào, "shine; illuminate") involves the projection of light, which can be linked to the notion of providing attention and insight, as if 'shining a light' on someone; this connects to the idea of looking after. 顾(顧) (gù, "look after; attend to") literally means turning the head to look, indicating focus and care. Together, 照顾(照顧) (zhàogù, "take care of; look after") conveys the concept of paying careful attention to someone or something, as if continually illuminating and tending to it with care.

parts.english: look after; attend to; care for

examples.chinese: 妈妈每天都照顾我(媽媽每天都照顧我)。
examples.english: Mom takes care of me every day.

examples.chinese: 请多多照顾新来的同事(請多多照顧新來的同事)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng duōduō zhàogù xīn lái de tóngshì.
examples.english: Please take good care of the new colleague.

examples.chinese: 他生病了，需要有人照顾他(他生病了，需要有人照顧他)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shēngbìng le, xūyào yǒurén zhàogù tā.
examples.english: He is sick and needs someone to take care of him.
